sections.heading: Hardware Overview
sections.content: NEXUS hardware consists of two USB cameras for stereo vision and an ESP32 DevKit that controls a pan/tilt servo mechanism with an attached laser module. The ESP32 communicates with NEXUS over USB-Serial at 115200 baud using a custom text-based protocol. All hardware communication is handled by the TacticalSerial class in core/hardware_link.py.

sections.heading: Hardware Components
rows: Requires external 5V/2A power supply — do not power from ESP32 5V
Controlled via ESP32 GPIO — TTL high/low
Optional — B1/B0 commands in firmware v2.2

sections.heading: ESP32 GPIO Wiring
rows: Orange/Yellow signal wire; Red→5V ext; Brown/Black→GND
High = laser ON, Low = laser OFF
Servo GND, Laser GND, Buzzer GND
All GND lines must share common ground with ESP32
Use external regulator — servos draw too much current for onboard 5V

sections.heading: ESP32 Firmware
sections.content: The ESP32 runs nexus_guardian_v2.2.ino which accepts serial commands, drives servos via hardware PWM, controls the laser GPIO, and streams a heartbeat for connection health monitoring.
sections.code:
# Serial Command Format (sent from NEXUS → ESP32)
P{pan}T{tilt}L{laser}\n

# Examples:
P90.0T150.0L1\n    → Pan=90°, Tilt=150°, Laser ON
P45.5T120.0L0\n    → Pan=45.5°, Tilt=120°, Laser OFF
B1\n               → Buzzer ON
B0\n               → Buzzer OFF

# ESP32 Responses:
ACK:{pan},{tilt}   → Confirms command received and executed
HB:{pan},{tilt},{laser}  → Heartbeat emitted every 2 seconds automatically

sections.heading: Serial Protocol Details
rows: Firmware enforces this — faster commands are ignored
Emitted by ESP32 automatically — used by TacticalSerial to detect disconnection
Center — sent on startup and safety timeout
Slightly downward — sent on startup and safety timeout

sections.heading: TacticalSerial (core/hardware_link.py)
sections.content: The TacticalSerial class provides thread-safe serial communication with the ESP32. It runs a background monitoring thread that detects disconnections and attempts auto-reconnect every 5 seconds.

sections.heading: Auto-Reconnect Behavior
sections.content: The TacticalSerial background monitor thread tracks connection health using heartbeat timing. If no heartbeat is received within 6 seconds, the port is flagged as disconnected and reconnect attempts begin automatically.
list: Detection: Heartbeat timeout after 6 seconds of silence marks connection as lost
Force reconnect: Dashboard RECONNECT button calls force_reconnect() to bypass cooldown
Laser state restore: On successful reconnect, the last known laser intent is automatically re-sent
Home on disconnect: Servos return to home position (Pan=90°, Tilt=150°) when connection is lost

sections.heading: Flashing ESP32 Firmware
sections.content: The ESP32 must have the NEXUS firmware flashed before any hardware communication works. Flash using Arduino IDE 2.x.
steps: Open Arduino IDE 2.x (download from arduino.cc if needed)
Add ESP32 board support: File → Preferences → Additional Boards Manager URLs → paste https://raw.githubusercontent.com/espressif/arduino-esp32/gh-pages/package_esp32_index.json
Install board: Tools → Boards Manager → search esp32 → install esp32 by Espressif Systems
Open firmware/nexus_guardian_v2.2.ino from the NEXUS project folder
Select Tools → Port: the CH340 COM port (e.g., COM3, COM8)
Open Serial Monitor at 115200 baud — you should see heartbeat lines: HB:90.0,150.0,0

sections.heading: Hardware Control Tab (tab_hardware.py)
sections.content: The Hardware Control tab provides manual control over servos and the laser without running the full AI engine. Useful for testing wiring, verifying servo ranges, and collecting laser alignment points.
list: Manual Pan Slider: Move pan servo from 0° to 180° manually
Manual Tilt Slider: Move tilt servo from 0° to 180° manually
Laser Toggle Button: Turn laser on/off independently of the AI engine
Send to Home: Immediately send Pan=90°, Tilt=150° home position
Sweep Test: Automatically sweep pan from 0° to 180° and back — verifies full servo range
Connect / Disconnect: Manually open or close the serial connection to the ESP32
Serial Monitor: Live view of raw serial traffic (commands sent + ACK/HB received)

sections.heading: Connection Troubleshooting
sections.alertContent: If hardware fails to connect: (1) Verify correct COM port in Settings — check Device Manager → Ports (COM & LPT). (2) Install CH340 driver from WCH if the port doesn't appear. (3) Ensure no other application (Arduino IDE Serial Monitor, PuTTY, another NEXUS instance) is using the same COM port. (4) Try a different USB cable — some cables are charge-only and don't carry data. (5) Reboot the ESP32 by pressing its RESET button while NEXUS is running.

sections.heading: Power Supply Requirements
sections.content: Incorrect power supply is the most common cause of erratic servo behavior (jitter, weak movement, or no movement).
rows: Same external supply as pan servo
Powered via USB from NEXUS PC

sections.heading: Camera Physical Setup
sections.content: Camera placement directly affects stereo triangulation accuracy. Follow these guidelines for optimal depth estimation:
list: Baseline distance: 6–12 cm between camera centers is ideal for targets at 0.5–5m range. Wider baseline = better far-distance depth but worse near-distance accuracy
Parallel alignment: Both cameras must point in exactly the same direction. Use a rigid mounting bar or 3D-printed bracket
Same optical axis height: Camera centers should be at identical heights — vertical misalignment adds y-disparity error
No toe-in: Both cameras must be parallel, NOT angled toward each other. Stereo calibration handles small misalignments but not deliberate toe-in
Rigid mount: Any flex between cameras invalidates calibration. Tighten all screws. Re-calibrate if the mount is disturbed
USB cable routing: Keep USB cables from both cameras at the same length to avoid one pulling on the mount
